{{Infobox concept
| name = Tartarus steel
| introduced = The Crimson Vault
}}

''See also: [[Tartarus steel Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus]] [[steel]] is an exceptionally rare and expensive metal characterized by its mirror-like shine. When forged and treated correctly, the material is nearly indestructible and can easily pierce standard metals. While it is highly durable, blades made of this substance can still clash with one another without breaking. This metal is used to craft various items, including massive war hammers, swords, and even padlocks. Additionally, [[Tartarus Travelers]] utilize small silver-colored keys made of this [[steel]] to facilitate their summoning abilities. The material is also used to create various summoned objects, such as needles, spinning blades, and metallic creatures.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus]] [[steel]] is a mirror-bright material used to forge [[Indirial]]'s war-hammer. This nearly unbreakable substance serves as a specialized blade material and is utilized to craft summoning keys for [[Tartarus Travelers]]. Additionally, the metal is employed to create a soldier's shining [[sword]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus steel]] is a material used to make [[Indirial]]'s war-hammer.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c3">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus steel]] is all but unbreakable.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c4">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus steel]] is used to make summoning keys for [[Tartarus Travelers]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus steel]] is a type of blade material.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus steel]] is mirror-bright.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Tartarus steel]] is used to make a soldier's shining [[sword]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c20" />}}

== City of Light ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Tartarus steel]] is a material used to cover a giant.<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c14">''City of Light'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Indirial]] is working with a pile of [[Tartarus steel]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c21">''City of Light'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|[[Erastes]] holds a [[Tartarus steel]] infantry [[sword]].<ref name="tg-03-city-of-light-c25">''City of Light'', chapter 25</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:City of Light]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate concepts]]
